events.description: Created Slenderman character on Something Awful forums
events.source: Something Awful forums thread

events.description: Earliest documented mirror ritual variants appear in 19th century American folklore, linked to divination practices for unmarried women seeking to see future husbands
events.source: Folklore studies — documented in multiple 19th century American almanacs and parlor game collections

events.description: Disturbances reported by John Bell family in Adams, Tennessee. First documented account published by Martin V. Ingram in 1894 based on alleged diary of Richard Williams Bell
events.source: Ingram, M.V. — Authenticated History of the Bell Witch (1894)

events.description: First reported sightings near Resurrection Cemetery, Justice Illinois. Multiple independent accounts from cab drivers and motorists documented through 1930s-1970s
events.source: Chicago folklore records — documented by folklorist Richard Crowe

events.description: Mac Brazel discovers debris near Roswell, New Mexico. Roswell Army Air Field issues a press release (flying disc) then withdraws it within 24 hours; replacement narrative attributes debris to weather balloon
events.source: Roswell Daily Record — July 8, 1947; concurrent military statements in public record

events.description: Erich von Däniken publishes Chariots of the Gods? attributing select ancient monuments and texts to extraterrestrial visitation
events.source: von Däniken — Chariots of the Gods? (1968), original German edition Auf den Spuren der All-Mächtigen

events.description: Vlad III born; Wallachian voivode whose life is documented in Ottoman, Hungarian, and Wallachian chronicles; later literary linkage to vampire fiction is separate from the historical record
events.source: Ottoman and Wallachian documentary record; academic historiography

events.description: Austrian administration produces Visum et Repertum (1722) documenting exhumation and popular belief in vampire revenants in Serbian locale; story syndicated in European newspapers of the period
events.source: Visum et Repertum (1722); contemporary press reproductions (e.g. Wienerisches Diarium)

events.description: Pyramid Texts in the pyramid of Unas at Saqqara (Old Kingdom, approx. late 24th century BCE) document the death, resurrection, and kingship of Osiris — among the oldest extensive religious compositions on the historic record
events.source: Pyramid Texts — Unas pyramid complex, Saqqara, Egypt (c. 2400–2300 BCE, scholarly dating ranges vary)

events.description: First widely cited sightings of a large winged humanoid figure near Clendenin and Point Pleasant, West Virginia; ongoing coverage in local press (Point Pleasant Register) November–December 1966
events.source: Point Pleasant Register — November 1966; regional newspaper record

events.description: First reported attacks in and around London (often dated from autumn 1837); complaint letters addressed to the Lord Mayor; accounts widely reprinted in metropolitan press including The Times in 1838
events.source: The Times (London) — 1838; Lord Mayor correspondence (contested copies in record)

events.description: Private pilot Kenneth Arnold reports nine crescent-shaped objects near Mount Rainier, Washington; wire services and national press coverage popularized the term flying saucers for disc-like craft
events.source: Associated Press and contemporaneous U.S. press — June 1947

events.description: Rancher Mac Brazel reports debris on his property near Roswell, New Mexico; material reported to local sheriff and Army Air Forces; chain leads to Roswell Army Air Field press release and rapid retraction
events.source: Roswell Daily Record — July 8, 1947; public statement record

events.description: New Hampshire couple reports a puzzling road encounter and missing time; later hypnotic regression sessions (documented from 1964) produce elaborated abduction narrative that becomes the first widely publicized alien abduction account in U.S. mass media
events.source: John Fuller — The Interrupted Journey (1966); contemporaneous coverage of book and lectures

events.description: Habsburg-era investigation of alleged vampire revenant Peter Plogojowitz in Kisiljevo (Serbian territory under Austrian administration); imperial authorities document exhumation inquiry among earliest formal European vampire dossiers
events.source: Austrian Imperial Court / military administrative report — 1725

events.description: Family and community in Exeter, Rhode Island exhume Mercy Brown’s body; heart removed and burned per folk remedial practice against alleged consumptive vampire; reported in Providence press
events.source: Providence Journal — March 19, 1892

events.description: Feathered-serpent deity and cultural complex attested from Classic-period Mesoamerican iconography (e.g. Teotihuacan, c. 900 CE onward in scholarly dating); later Aztec state cult and textual sources; Toltec ruler Ce Acatl Topiltzin syncretized with the deity in post-Classic narrative
events.source: Pre-Columbian codices, inscriptions, and archaeological publication record

events.description: Akan (Ashanti) spider-trickster figure documented in published ethnographic collection; oral narrative corpus predates Rattray’s compilation and spread with diaspora into Caribbean and African-American story traditions
events.source: R.S. Rattray — Ashanti (1923); anthropological and folklore secondary literature

events.description: Titan who steals fire for humanity and suffers punishment; narrative attested in Hesiod’s Theogony (c. 7th century BCE); Prometheus Bound and other classical sources expand torment at Zeus’s order — attested across multiple ancient Greek textual witnesses
events.source: Hesiod — Theogony (c. 700 BCE); classical Greek dramatic and poetic corpus

events.description: Gorgon figure named in Hesiod’s Theogony (c. 700 BCE); Roman Ovid Metamorphoses (8 CE) reworks myth with Poseidon encounter and Athena transformation — documentary shift between Greek archaic and Latin imperial literary layers
events.source: Hesiod — Theogony (c. 700 BCE); Ovid — Metamorphoses (8 CE)

events.description: Hindu goddess of destruction, time, and fierce protection; textual prominence in Devi Mahatmya / Markandeya Purana stratum (scholarly dating c. 400–600 CE, often cited ~500 CE); earlier South Asian iconography is debated in scholarship and is not a 1:1 match to later Kali imagery in all sources
events.source: Devi Mahatmya / Markandeya Purana tradition (c. 500 CE scholarly consensus range); Indus Valley and pre-Gupta archaeology (interpretive literature)

events.description: Algonquian-language-area spirit associated with winter and cannibalistic transformation; ethnographic attestation in Ojibwe, Cree, and Innu traditions. First widely cited European-written note: Jesuit Relations (Paul Le Jeune, 1636). Tier documents existence of the belief in the record, not verification of supernatural claims; missionary source carries colonial observer bias
events.source: Jesuit Relations — Paul Le Jeune, 1636; later ethnography

events.description: Reporter Brian Bethel’s narrative of black-eyed child hitchhikers posted to an online paranormal discussion list (Abilene Reporter-News journalist); story documented as early networked folklore seed and traced through forum archives
events.source: Brian Bethel — original 1996 internet post (Abilene Reporter-News attribution in later coverage)

events.description: Collaborative fiction wiki presenting imaginary anomalies in bureaucratic / case-file tone; launched on community model in 2008 (English-speaking creepypasta scene); publicly mistaken by some readers for leaked government documentation
events.source: SCP Foundation wiki — community genesis and site history (scp-wiki.net, 2008)
